name: Pt. Leo Estate
signature: A sculpture park walk followed by a long bay-view lunch in the glass-walled restaurant
editorNote:
Pt. Leo is the Peninsula's great ambition made real: 93 hectares where you can walk among large-scale sculptures before sitting down to a two-hat lunch with one of the best bay views in the state. The setting alone is worth the drive.

The restaurant handles the produce-forward brief with real skill. The wine list is exceptional  -  Peninsula estate wines alongside a sharp selection of European bottles. Book the terrace facing Port Phillip for the full effect.

Arrive for the sculpture walk before noon. Give yourself half a day. This is not a quick lunch.

visiting.tastingNote: Walk-ins welcome for groups up to 6. Four tasting tiers: Standard $15, Red $25, White $25, Premium $30 — all include 5 wines; $10 refundable on bottle purchase. Laura restaurant requires advance booking.

restaurant.name: Laura
restaurant.description: Laura holds 2 Hats from the Good Food Guide. The main Pt. Leo Restaurant offers Modern Australian casual dining; the Wine Terrace handles walk-in visits.

editorVerdict: Pt. Leo Estate operates at a scale that few Peninsula producers can match: the Gandel family's investment has produced a world-class outdoor sculpture park, a fine-dining restaurant in Laura (2 hats), and a cellar door running four tasting tiers. The Rosé took Best Rosé at the Mornington Peninsula Wine Show in both 2024 and 2025. It's a destination property, and it knows it.

faq.q: Does Pt. Leo Estate take walk-ins or do you need to book?
faq.a: Walk-ins are welcome at the cellar door for groups of up to 6 guests. Laura and the main restaurant require advance bookings.

faq.q: Does Pt. Leo Estate have a restaurant?
faq.a: Yes — two. Laura (2 Hats, Good Food Guide) for fine dining, and Pt. Leo Restaurant for Modern Australian main dining, plus the casual Wine Terrace.

faq.q: Are dogs allowed at Pt. Leo Estate?
faq.a: No. Only on-duty Guide Dogs and certified service animals are permitted on the property.

heroImage.alt: Pt. Leo Estate restaurant terrace with Port Phillip Bay views and sculpture park
